title: Archer Aviation Q1 2026 Earnings: Beat Expectations Despite Stock Decline
text: Archer Aviation surpassed analyst forecasts in Q1 2026, reporting an EPS of -$0.28 versus the predicted -$0.30, a 6.67% positive surprise. Revenue reached $1.6 million, exceeding the $1.54 million forecast by 3.9%. The company maintains exceptional financial health with $1.8 billion in liquidity and minimal debt under $100 million. The current ratio stands at 19.89 with a debt-to-equity ratio of just 0.06, providing substantial flexibility for strategic initiatives. Despite beating earnings targets, ACHR stock declined 1.23% in aftermarket trading, closing at $6.40. The stock exhibits high volatility with a beta of 3.13, posting a 13% gain over the past week while down 27% over six months. CEO Adam Goldstein emphasized that the strong liquidity position allows the company to fund strategic priorities without leverage constraints. The company projects adjusted EBITDA losses of $170-$200 million for Q2 2026, reflecting increased investment in civil, defense, and AI software platforms. Archer became the first eVTOL company to close phase 3 of the FAA's four-phase certification process and is advancing phase 4. The company is developing a hybrid autonomous aircraft with Anduril, targeting defense procurement opportunities with projected government awards later in 2026. Operations at Hawthorne Airport in Los Angeles continue advancing air taxi capabilities for the 2028 Olympics.
